source: Live web - outbreak, security & travel status (seeded snapshot)

items.headline: WHO declares cholera outbreak in Am Timan, Sila Province, Chad
items.detail: Rapidly rising case counts around Am Timan; WHO and health cluster calling for immediate epidemiology, WASH, and case-management surge. French-speaking teams required. Yellow Fever and cholera vaccination mandatory for the zone.

items.headline: Security advisory: West Darfur, Sudan escalated to Level 4 - no non-essential travel
items.detail: Armed clashes near El Fasher; movement severely restricted. Staff currently in the El Fasher area cannot safely depart for cross-border missions.

items.headline: Sudan-Chad border closed at Adre crossing
items.detail: The Adre land crossing between Sudan and eastern Chad is closed until further notice. No overland transit between Sudan and Chad; no commercial flights out of El Fasher before 7 July.

items.headline: N'Djamena international hub operational; Cameroon-Chad corridor open
items.detail: N'Djamena airport operating normally with connections from Yaounde and Maroua. The Cameroon-Chad road corridor via Kousseri is open and passable; onward road access toward Am Timan reported usable.

items.headline: Regional weather: seasonal rains beginning across the Sahel
items.detail: Early rains may slow overland movement toward Sila Province later in the week; air-then-road via N'Djamena is the most reliable route into Am Timan right now.
